chapter goal: Build a working RP^d/RP^3 calculus where points, lines, planes, joins, meets, and elements at infinity are all handled by labeled homogeneous coordinates.
source span read: Perspectives on Projective Geometry, Chapter 12, sections 12.1-12.6; printed pages 209-226; PDF pages 231-248. Source map checked in AGENTS.md and ppg_inventory.py.
concept inventory: RP^3 as nonzero vectors in R^4 modulo nonzero scalar multiples.
Affine R^3 chart w=1 and the plane at infinity w=0.
Point-plane incidence as one homogeneous scalar equation.
Planes through three points and points from three planes by signed 3x3 minors.
Lines in RP^3 represented by six 2x2 minors with labels 12, 13, 14, 23, 24, 34.
Line coordinates are decomposable exactly when the Grassmann-Pluecker relation l12*l34 - l13*l24 + l14*l23 = 0 holds.
Universal join and meet use subset labels Lambda(d,k), not positional vector entries.
Join/meet zero outputs detect dependence or incidence: point on line, point on plane, coplanar points, coincident planes, or parallel planes meeting at infinity.
A line at infinity can be obtained by joining two infinite points or by meeting two parallel finite planes.

library routing table.concept: RP^3 chart plus plane at infinity
library routing table.representation: interactive 3D Plotly scene with finite points, direction rays, an affine plane, and the plane-at-infinity proxy
library routing table.why this library: RP^3 and line/plane incidence need 3D inspection and camera rotation; standalone HTML keeps the lab durable after execution.

library routing table.representation: labeled static diagram linking two finite points, their 2x2 minors, and the finite/infinite split of labels
library routing table.why this library: A labeled 2D explanatory panel is more readable than a dense 3D plot for the six coordinates.
library routing table.fallback: Markdown table plus numeric vector display

library routing table.representation: symbolic exact check and numeric residual table for a true line, a perturbed non-line vector, and an infinite line
library routing table.why this library: The relation is an algebraic identity, so exact symbolic arithmetic and a compact table expose the invariant directly.

library routing table.representation: dependency/lattice diagram of ranks 0..4 and allowed operations in RP^3
library routing table.why this library: The chapter changes from positional vectors to subset labels; a graph of rank movement makes the algebraic bookkeeping inspectable.

library routing table.representation: camera-projection HTML that projects a 3D line and a point at infinity to an image plane
library routing table.why this library: Projective d-space motivates matrix transformations and projection; an inspectable 3D scene shows finite and infinite objects together.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Rotate the scene and compare finite points w=1 with direction points w=0; the latter live on the plane-at-infinity proxy.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: finite points dehomogenize; infinite points raise the expected chart exception and have zero w-coordinate.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Check which finite points lie on the plane, which fail the scalar equation, and how transformed planes preserve incidence.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: plane dot point is zero for incident points and remains zero after applying T to points and inverse-transpose to planes.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Read each label as a 2x2 minor and identify the three labels that vanish for a line at infinity in the standard embedding.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: line computed from two different point pairs on the same line is a scalar multiple.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Compare decomposable and perturbed six-vectors; only decomposable vectors have zero relation residual.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: l12*l34 - l13*l24 + l14*l23 equals zero for joins of two points and nonzero for the perturbed vector.

visual sequence.learner inspection target: Track how join increases rank and meet decreases dual rank, including number-valued incidence tests.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: computed operation ranks match the RP^3 join/meet table.

visual sequence.concept: parallel planes and line at infinity
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Compare the meet of two parallel planes with the join of two infinite direction points.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: both constructions return proportional line coordinates with labels 14, 24, and 34 equal to zero.

visual sequence.representation: interactive projection scene plus JSON check
visual sequence.learner inspection target: Move from 3D object coordinates to a projective image plane and inspect when a spatial direction becomes a vanishing point.
visual sequence.validation/invariant: projected image points satisfy homogeneous scaling and a line projects to a line.

computational checks: Exact SymPy expansion of the Grassmann-Pluecker relation for p join q.
Numeric residuals for a decomposable line, a same-line resampling, an infinite line, and a perturbed six-vector.
Point-plane incidence and incidence preservation under T and inverse-transpose(T).
Line-plane meet returns a point on both the line and plane.
Meet of parallel planes is proportional to join of two infinite direction points.
Universal join/meet rank labels match the RP^3 operation table.
Projection lab maps collinear 3D points to collinear image points.
All chapter artifacts exist under the assigned subtree and have nonzero size.
proof-visualization strategy: Use labeled exterior-coordinate panels and a rank lattice instead of copied proof text. The notebook scaffolds the multilinearity/antisymmetry proof idea by recomputing a line from two different point pairs and by showing the decomposability residual before and after perturbation.
implementation notes: Do not edit shared utilities; implement small chapter-local functions inside the notebook.
Keep paths relative to BOOK_ROOT and write only the assigned chapter artifact subtree.
Use Plotly for the 3D RP^3 and projection labs, Matplotlib/NetworkX for labeled static diagrams, SymPy for exact identities, and pandas for check tables.
Display every artifact near the concept it teaches; final sanity checks reload JSON/CSV artifacts and assert invariants.
risks: Plotly HTML is durable, but static PNG export from Plotly may require optional browser/kaleido support; avoid requiring it.
Projective objects are scale classes, so all numeric equality checks must compare proportional representatives or residuals, not raw coordinate equality.
Remove stale template artifacts so the revised notebook relies only on the chapter-specific RP^3, Pluecker-line, and join/meet checks.
acceptance criteria: Notebook contains title, source span, chapter goal, computational translation guide, concept sections, applied lab, takeaways, and final sanity checks.
Notebook text and artifacts explicitly include RP^d/RP^3, points, planes, lines, six Pluecker coordinates, Grassmann-Pluecker relation, and universal join/meet.
At least five concept-named artifacts are generated under artifacts/chapter-12-projective-d-space.
Checks assert Pluecker relation, incidence/join/meet behavior, RP^3 projection behavior, artifact existence, and nonzero artifact size.
The canonical notebook executes with nbclient from the chapter folder or course root.
Scoped git diff --check passes for the assigned notebook and artifact subtree.
